expectations.text: Presents organization system options to the user (mentions PARA, MOC, flat+tags, or Johnny Decimal)
expectations.evidence: Agent explicitly stated: 'Which organization system do you prefer? Options are: PARA, Maps of Content (MOC), Flat + Tags, or Johnny Decimal.' All four systems were mentioned.

expectations.text: Recommends PARA as the default for the described use case
expectations.evidence: Agent stated: 'Since I'm told to proceed with the default, I'll use PARA — it's the skill's default recommendation and works well for a software engineer who tracks projects and takes meeting notes. PARA separates active projects from ongoing areas and reference material, which maps naturally to engineering work.'

expectations.text: Creates folder structure with at minimum: Projects, Areas/Resources, Archive, templates, daily-notes, and attachments directories
expectations.evidence: Agent ran: mkdir -p /tmp/test-vault/{1-Projects,2-Areas,3-Resources,4-Archive,templates,daily-notes,attachments} — all required directories were created.

expectations.text: Creates an index/home note with wikilinks ([[...]]) linking to key areas
expectations.evidence: Home.md contains wikilinks to all key areas: [[1-Projects/]], [[2-Areas/]], [[3-Resources/]], [[4-Archive/]], [[templates/Daily Note Template|Daily Note Template]], [[templates/Meeting Notes Template|Meeting Notes Template]].

expectations.text: Creates a daily note template with YAML frontmatter (--- fenced) including at least tags and date fields
expectations.evidence: Daily Note Template.md has YAML frontmatter fenced with --- containing 'tags: [daily]' and 'date: "{{date:YYYY-MM-DD}}"'.

expectations.text: Creates a meeting notes template with YAML frontmatter including at least tags, date, and attendees fields
expectations.evidence: Meeting Notes Template.md has YAML frontmatter with 'tags: [meeting]', 'date: "{{date:YYYY-MM-DD}}"', and 'attendees: []'.

expectations.text: All internal links use wikilink syntax [[...]] not markdown link syntax []()
expectations.evidence: Grep for markdown-style links [text](url) across all three output files returned zero matches. All internal links use [[...]] wikilink syntax (e.g., [[1-Projects/]], [[Home]]).

expectations.text: Templates use proper Obsidian template variables like {{date}} or {{title}}
expectations.evidence: Both templates use {{date:YYYY-MM-DD}} and the daily note template also uses {{date:dddd}} for day-of-week. These are proper Obsidian Templater/core template variable syntax.
